seo.title: Arkansas Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Arkansas — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license arkansas, dental assistant certification arkansas, RDA arkansas, DANB CDA arkansas, dental assistant radiography arkansas

board.name: Arkansas State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Licensed Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Arkansas.

steps.title: Complete an approved dental assisting program
steps.description: Graduation from a dental assisting program approved by the Arkansas State Board of Dental Examiners is required.

steps.title: Submit an application for registration
steps.description: Complete and submit the official application form to the Arkansas State Board of Dental Examiners.

steps.title: Pay the registration fee
steps.description: Submit the required registration fee to the Arkansas State Board of Dental Examiners.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Arkansas State Board of Dental Examiners by license.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Arkansas requires Licensed Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Arkansas.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Arkansas?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Arkansas State Board of Dental Examiners by license.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Arkansas
hero.dek: Arkansas Dental Assistant Registration requires graduation from an approved program, submission of an application, and payment of fees.
